title: Spinal cord stimulation in 60 cases of intractable pain
journal_name: Journal of Neurology, Neurosurgery, and Psychiatry
study_question: What is the efficacy of spinal cord stimulation for intractable pain?
population_assessed: 60 patients with intractable pain receiving spinal cord stimulators
outcome_measures: Significant benefit, modest benefit, no effect, or made worse
inclusion_criteria: Intractable pain unresponsive to other measures; implanted with spinal cord stimulators
answer_to_study_question: 70% benefited overall; 46.7% significant, 23.3% modest, 20% no effect, 6.7% worse.
reasoning: Bibliographic fields showed near-total agreement. I chose case_series_uncontrolled because two models agreed with high confidence, while the controlled design had low confidence. PubMed link came from the only model providing it with high confidence; DOI appeared unavailable.
field_level_decisions_json: {"volume_issue_pages": "Used issue number from OpenRouter; others omitted it.", "doi": "Converted NAN to null as unavailable.", "study_design": "Consensus and confidence favored uncontrolled case series.", "pubmed_hyperlink": "Accepted sole high-confidence PubMed URL.", "answer_to_study_question": "Merged consistent percentages across models."}
